title: glyceria acutiflora subsp. japonica
intro: Glyceria acutiflora subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese fescue or Japanese mannagrass, is a type of grass native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Glyceria acutiflora subsp. japonica include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
